result:
All changes are in place and validated with a full `next build` + local smoke test (this workspace sits under `results/`, which is gitignored at the harness repo root — expected, not an issue). Summary of what I found and fixed:

**Root cause:** the site is mid-pivot from an old product ("InboxZap," an email-cleanup tool) to the current one ("Lumina," an AI meeting assistant), and the pivot left several SEO-breaking loose ends:

1. **Brand mismatch in metadata** — `<title>`/description/OG still said "InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes" while every page was about Lumina. Google's search snippet promised one product; visitors landed on another. Rewrote to accurately describe Lumina, and removed a reference to an OG image that didn't exist in `public/`.
2. **Broken nav & CTA** — `/features` and `/pricing` were linked from the nav (and the homepage's only CTA) but 404'd. Built both out with real content.
3. **Dead `/faq` redirect** — `next.config.js` 301s `/faq` → `/#faq`, but no FAQ section existed. Added one, plus FAQPage JSON-LD for rich-result eligibility.
4. **Blog was fully non-functional** — the index page was client-rendered and fetched `/api/posts`, an endpoint that doesn't exist anywhere in the app, so it always showed "Could not load posts." Worse, GSC shows `/blog/post-1`…`/blog/post-12` still pulling thousands of monthly impressions each, but there was no `[slug]` route at all — pure 404s, burning previously-indexed equity. Rebuilt the blog as SSR, and reused those exact 12 URLs with new, genuinely on-topic content (meeting notes, action items, transcription, meeting fatigue, etc.) instead of the old off-brand inbox-cleanup topics the GSC queries showed they used to rank for.
5. **Incomplete sitemap** — listed `/features`/`/pricing` before they existed and explicitly excluded blog posts via a TODO. Now dynamically includes every real page and post.
6. Bumped `next` 14.2.5 → 14.2.35 (same minor line, no breaking changes) — 14.2.5 had a critical DoS/cache-poisoning advisory open; a takedown zeroes out organic traffic regardless of content quality.

One judgment call worth flagging: I didn't try to resurrect the old GSC-heavy queries (bulk-delete emails, gmail storage, unsubscribe newsletters) since they're about a product Lumina no longer offers — chasing that traffic would mean off-brand content with no conversion path. Redirected that intent implicitly by giving the same URLs fresh, relevant content instead.
